title: goodenia hederacea subsp. alpestris
intro: Goodenia hederacea subsp. alpestris, also known as Alpine Goodenia, is a subspecies of plant native to Australia, characterized by its delicate blue or purple flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, Goodenia hederacea subsp. alpestris has been used in herbal medicine for its purported anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and infections. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Goodenia hederacea subsp. alpestris is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
